network_policies.name: spark-operator-allow-internal
issues: policyTypes does not include Egress (all egress traffic is allowed)
No USER directive found (defaults to root)

webhooks.name: mutate-pod.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that intercepts Pod create/update requests in Spark job namespaces, looks up the owning SparkApplication by label, and defaults the pod spec with settings from the SparkApplication CR—including volumes, environment variables, config maps, affinity, tolerations, node selectors, security contexts, GPU resources, sidecar/init containers, owner references, ports, memory limits, and scheduling configuration—differentiating between driver and executor pods.

webhooks.name: mutate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Mutating webhook that sets default values on SparkApplication resources during create and update operations. It only applies defaults (via the operator's webhook scheme) when the application is in a 'New' or 'Invalidating' state, ensuring that defaults are not re-applied to already-running or completed applications.

webhooks.name: validate-scheduledsparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validates ScheduledSparkApplication resources on create and update, ensuring the metadata.name is a valid DNS-1035 label so that downstream Kubernetes Services and SparkApplication resources derived from the scheduled app can be created without naming failures.

webhooks.name: validate-sparkapplication.sparkoperator.k8s.io
webhooks.purpose: Validating webhook for SparkApplication resources on create and update. It enforces that the application name is DNS-1035 compliant (to prevent downstream Service creation failures), prevents conflicting NodeSelector definitions at both the application and driver/executor levels, validates DriverIngressOptions for duplicate service ports or ingress URL formats, requires Spark >= 3.0.0 for pod template features, and optionally checks that requested resources do not exceed namespace ResourceQuotas.

ingress_routing.name: rbac-inferred
ingress_routing.note: component has RBAC permissions for this resource type but no static manifests found

api_types.name: DynamicAllocation
api_types.doc: DynamicAllocation contains configuration options for dynamic allocation.

fields.name: Enabled
fields.doc: Enabled controls whether dynamic allocation is enabled or not.

fields.name: InitialExecutors
fields.doc: InitialExecutors is the initial number of executors to request. If .spec.executor.instances is also set, the initial number of executors is set to the bigger of that and this option.

fields.name: MinExecutors
fields.doc: MinExecutors is the lower bound for the number of executors if dynamic allocation is enabled.

fields.name: MaxExecutors
fields.doc: MaxExecutors is the upper bound for the number of executors if dynamic allocation is enabled.

fields.name: ShuffleTrackingEnabled
fields.doc: ShuffleTrackingEnabled enables shuffle file tracking for executors, which allows dynamic allocation without the need for an external shuffle service. This option will try to keep alive executors that are storing shuffle data for active jobs. If external shuffle service is enabled, set ShuffleTrackingEnabled to false. ShuffleTrackingEnabled is true by default if dynamicAllocation.enabled is true.

fields.name: ShuffleTrackingTimeout
fields.doc: ShuffleTrackingTimeout controls the timeout in milliseconds for executors that are holding shuffle data if shuffle tracking is enabled (true by default if dynamic allocation is enabled).

api_types.name: SparkPodSpec
api_types.doc: SparkPodSpec defines common things that can be customized for a Spark driver or executor pod.

fields.name: Cores
fields.doc: Cores maps to `spark.driver.cores` or `spark.executor.cores` for the driver and executors, respectively.

fields.name: Memory
fields.doc: Memory is the amount of memory to request for the pod.

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is a pod template that can be used to define the driver or executor pod configurations that Spark configurations do not support. Spark version >= 3.0.0 is required. Ref: https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/running-on-kubernetes.html#pod-template.

api_types.name: SparkConnectStatus
api_types.doc: SparkConnectStatus defines the observed state of SparkConnect.

fields.name: Conditions
fields.doc: Represents the latest available observations of a SparkConnect's current state. +patchMergeKey=type +patchStrategy=merge +listType=map +listMapKey=type

fields.name: State
fields.doc: State represents the current state of the SparkConnect.

fields.name: Server
fields.doc: Server represents the current state of the SparkConnect server.

fields.name: Executors
fields.doc: Executors represents the current state of the SparkConnect executors.

fields.name: StartTime
fields.doc: StartTime is the time at which the SparkConnect controller started processing the SparkConnect.

fields.name: LastUpdateTime
fields.doc: LastUpdateTime is the time at which the SparkConnect controller last updated the SparkConnect.

fields.name: PodName
fields.doc: PodName is the name of the pod that is running the Spark Connect server.

fields.name: PodIP
fields.doc: PodIP is the IP address of the pod that is running the Spark Connect server.

fields.name: ServiceName
fields.doc: ServiceName is the name of the service that is exposing the Spark Connect server.

api_types.name: SparkConnect
api_types.doc: SparkConnect is the Schema for the sparkconnections API.

api_types.name: SparkConnectList
api_types.doc: SparkConnectList contains a list of SparkConnect.

api_types.name: SparkConnectSpec
api_types.doc: SparkConnectSpec defines the desired state of SparkConnect.

fields.name: SparkVersion
fields.doc: SparkVersion is the version of Spark the spark connect use.

fields.name: Image
fields.doc: Image is the container image for the driver, executor, and init-container. Any custom container images for the driver, executor, or init-container takes precedence over this.

fields.name: HadoopConf
fields.doc: HadoopConf carries user-specified Hadoop configuration properties as they would use the "--conf" option in spark-submit. The SparkApplication controller automatically adds prefix "spark.hadoop." to Hadoop configuration properties.

fields.name: SparkConf
fields.doc: SparkConf carries user-specified Spark configuration properties as they would use the "--conf" option in spark-submit.

fields.name: Server
fields.doc: Server is the Spark connect server specification.

fields.name: Executor
fields.doc: Executor is the Spark executor specification.

fields.name: DynamicAllocation
fields.doc: DynamicAllocation configures dynamic allocation that becomes available for the Kubernetes scheduler backend since Spark 3.0.

api_types.name: ServerSpec
api_types.doc: ServerSpec is specification of the Spark connect server.

fields.name: Service
fields.doc: Service exposes the Spark connect server.

api_types.name: ExecutorSpec
api_types.doc: ExecutorSpec is specification of the executor.

fields.name: Instances
fields.doc: Instances is the number of executor instances.

api_types.name: ScheduledSparkApplicationList
api_types.doc: ScheduledSparkApplicationList contains a list of ScheduledSparkApplication.

api_types.name: ScheduledSparkApplicationSpec
api_types.doc: ScheduledSparkApplicationSpec defines the desired state of ScheduledSparkApplication.

fields.name: Schedule
fields.doc: Schedule is a cron schedule on which the application should run.

fields.name: TimeZone
fields.doc: TimeZone is the time zone in which the cron schedule will be interpreted in. This value is passed to time.LoadLocation, so it must be either "Local", "UTC", or a valid IANA location name e.g. "America/New_York". Defaults to "Local".

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is a template from which SparkApplication instances can be created.

fields.name: Suspend
fields.doc: Suspend is a flag telling the controller to suspend subsequent runs of the application if set to true. Defaults to false.

fields.name: ConcurrencyPolicy
fields.doc: ConcurrencyPolicy is the policy governing concurrent SparkApplication runs.

fields.name: SuccessfulRunHistoryLimit
fields.doc: SuccessfulRunHistoryLimit is the number of past successful runs of the application to keep. Defaults to 1.

fields.name: FailedRunHistoryLimit
fields.doc: FailedRunHistoryLimit is the number of past failed runs of the application to keep. Defaults to 1.

api_types.name: ScheduledSparkApplicationStatus
api_types.doc: ScheduledSparkApplicationStatus defines the observed state of ScheduledSparkApplication.

fields.name: LastRun
fields.doc: LastRun is the time when the last run of the application started. +nullable

fields.name: NextRun
fields.doc: NextRun is the time when the next run of the application will start. +nullable

fields.name: LastRunName
fields.doc: LastRunName is the name of the SparkApplication for the most recent run of the application.

fields.name: PastSuccessfulRunNames
fields.doc: PastSuccessfulRunNames keeps the names of SparkApplications for past successful runs.

fields.name: PastFailedRunNames
fields.doc: PastFailedRunNames keeps the names of SparkApplications for past failed runs.

fields.name: ScheduleState
fields.doc: ScheduleState is the current scheduling state of the application.

fields.name: Reason
fields.doc: Reason tells why the ScheduledSparkApplication is in the particular ScheduleState.

api_types.name: ScheduledSparkApplication
api_types.doc: ScheduledSparkApplication is the Schema for the scheduledsparkapplications API.

api_types.name: SparkApplicationStatus
api_types.doc: SparkApplicationStatus defines the observed state of SparkApplication

fields.name: SparkApplicationID
fields.doc: SparkApplicationID is set by the spark-distribution(via spark.app.id config) on the driver and executor pods

fields.name: SubmissionID
fields.doc: SubmissionID is a unique ID of the current submission of the application.

fields.name: LastSubmissionAttemptTime
fields.doc: LastSubmissionAttemptTime is the time for the last application submission attempt. +nullable

fields.name: TerminationTime
fields.doc: CompletionTime is the time when the application runs to completion if it does. +nullable

fields.name: DriverInfo
fields.doc: DriverInfo has information about the driver.

fields.name: AppState
fields.doc: AppState tells the overall application state.

fields.name: ExecutorState
fields.doc: ExecutorState records the state of executors by executor Pod names.

fields.name: ExecutionAttempts
fields.doc: ExecutionAttempts is the total number of attempts to run a submitted application to completion. Incremented upon each attempted run of the application and reset upon invalidation.

fields.name: SubmissionAttempts
fields.doc: SubmissionAttempts is the total number of attempts to submit an application to run. Incremented upon each attempted submission of the application and reset upon invalidation and rerun.

api_types.name: SparkApplication
api_types.doc: SparkApplication is the Schema for the sparkapplications API

api_types.name: SparkApplicationList
api_types.doc: SparkApplicationList contains a list of SparkApplication

api_types.name: RestartPolicy
api_types.doc: RestartPolicy is the policy of if and in which conditions the controller should restart a terminated application. This completely defines actions to be taken on any kind of Failures during an application run.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type specifies the RestartPolicyType.

fields.name: OnSubmissionFailureRetries
fields.doc: OnSubmissionFailureRetries is the number of times to retry submitting an application before giving up. This is best effort and actual retry attempts can be >= the value specified due to caching. These are required if RestartPolicy is OnFailure.

fields.name: OnFailureRetries
fields.doc: OnFailureRetries the number of times to retry running an application before giving up.

fields.name: OnSubmissionFailureRetryInterval
fields.doc: OnSubmissionFailureRetryInterval is the interval in seconds between retries on failed submissions.

fields.name: OnFailureRetryInterval
fields.doc: OnFailureRetryInterval is the interval in seconds between retries on failed runs.

api_types.name: BatchSchedulerConfiguration
api_types.doc: BatchSchedulerConfiguration used to configure how to batch scheduling Spark Application

fields.name: Queue
fields.doc: Queue stands for the resource queue which the application belongs to, it's being used in Volcano batch scheduler.

fields.name: PriorityClassName
fields.doc: PriorityClassName stands for the name of k8s PriorityClass resource, it's being used in Volcano batch scheduler.

fields.name: Resources
fields.doc: Resources stands for the resource list custom request for. Usually it is used to define the lower-bound limit. If specified, volcano scheduler will consider it as the resources requested.

api_types.name: SparkUIConfiguration
api_types.doc: SparkUIConfiguration is for driver UI specific configuration parameters.

fields.name: ServicePort
fields.doc: ServicePort allows configuring the port at service level that might be different from the targetPort. TargetPort should be the same as the one defined in spark.ui.port

fields.name: ServicePortName
fields.doc: ServicePortName allows configuring the name of the service port. This may be useful for sidecar proxies like Envoy injected by Istio which require specific ports names to treat traffic as proper HTTP. Defaults to spark-driver-ui-port.

fields.name: ServiceType
fields.doc: ServiceType allows configuring the type of the service. Defaults to ClusterIP.

fields.name: ServiceAnnotations
fields.doc: ServiceAnnotations is a map of key,value pairs of annotations that might be added to the service object.

fields.name: ServiceLabels
fields.doc: ServiceLabels is a map of key,value pairs of labels that might be added to the service object.

fields.name: IngressAnnotations
fields.doc: IngressAnnotations is a map of key,value pairs of annotations that might be added to the ingress object. i.e. specify nginx as ingress.class

fields.name: IngressTLS
fields.doc: TlsHosts is useful If we need to declare SSL certificates to the ingress object

api_types.name: SparkApplicationSpec
api_types.doc: SparkApplicationSpec defines the desired state of SparkApplication It carries every pieces of information a spark-submit command takes and recognizes.

fields.name: Suspend
fields.doc: Suspend indicates whether the SparkApplication should be suspended. When true, the controller skips submitting the Spark job. If a SparkApplication is suspended after creation (i.e. the flag goes from false to true), the Spark operator will delete all active Pods associated with this SparkApplication. Users must design their Spark application to gracefully handle this.

fields.name: Type
fields.doc: Type tells the type of the Spark application.

fields.name: SparkVersion
fields.doc: SparkVersion is the version of Spark the application uses.

fields.name: Mode
fields.doc: Mode is the deployment mode of the Spark application.

fields.name: ProxyUser
fields.doc: ProxyUser specifies the user to impersonate when submitting the application. It maps to the command-line flag "--proxy-user" in spark-submit.

fields.name: Image
fields.doc: Image is the container image for the driver, executor, and init-container. Any custom container images for the driver, executor, or init-container takes precedence over this.

fields.name: ImagePullPolicy
fields.doc: ImagePullPolicy is the image pull policy for the driver, executor, and init-container.

fields.name: ImagePullSecrets
fields.doc: ImagePullSecrets is the list of image-pull secrets.

fields.name: MainClass
fields.doc: MainClass is the fully-qualified main class of the Spark application. This only applies to Java/Scala Spark applications.

fields.name: MainApplicationFile
fields.doc: MainFile is the path to a bundled JAR, Python, or R file of the application.

fields.name: Arguments
fields.doc: Arguments is a list of arguments to be passed to the application.

fields.name: SparkConf
fields.doc: SparkConf carries user-specified Spark configuration properties as they would use the  "--conf" option in spark-submit.

fields.name: HadoopConf
fields.doc: HadoopConf carries user-specified Hadoop configuration properties as they would use the "--conf" option in spark-submit. The SparkApplication controller automatically adds prefix "spark.hadoop." to Hadoop configuration properties.

fields.name: SparkConfigMap
fields.doc: SparkConfigMap carries the name of the ConfigMap containing Spark configuration files such as log4j.properties. The controller will add environment variable SPARK_CONF_DIR to the path where the ConfigMap is mounted to.

fields.name: HadoopConfigMap
fields.doc: HadoopConfigMap carries the name of the ConfigMap containing Hadoop configuration files such as core-site.xml. The controller will add environment variable HADOOP_CONF_DIR to the path where the ConfigMap is mounted to.

fields.name: Volumes
fields.doc: Volumes is the list of Kubernetes volumes that can be mounted by the driver and/or executors.

fields.name: Driver
fields.doc: Driver is the driver specification.

fields.name: Executor
fields.doc: Executor is the executor specification.

fields.name: Deps
fields.doc: Deps captures all possible types of dependencies of a Spark application.

fields.name: RestartPolicy
fields.doc: RestartPolicy defines the policy on if and in which conditions the controller should restart an application.

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector is the Kubernetes node selector to be added to the driver and executor pods. This field is mutually exclusive with nodeSelector at podSpec level (driver or executor). This field will be deprecated in future versions (at SparkApplicationSpec level).

fields.name: FailureRetries
fields.doc: FailureRetries is the number of times to retry a failed application before giving up. This is best effort and actual retry attempts can be >= the value specified.

fields.name: RetryInterval
fields.doc: RetryInterval is the unit of intervals in seconds between submission retries.

fields.name: PythonVersion
fields.doc: This sets the major Python version of the docker image used to run the driver and executor containers. Can either be 2 or 3, default 2.

fields.name: MemoryOverheadFactor
fields.doc: This sets the Memory Overhead Factor that will allocate memory to non-JVM memory. For JVM-based jobs this value will default to 0.10, for non-JVM jobs 0.40. Value of this field will be overridden by `Spec.Driver.MemoryOverhead` and `Spec.Executor.MemoryOverhead` if they are set.

fields.name: Monitoring
fields.doc: Monitoring configures how monitoring is handled.

fields.name: BatchScheduler
fields.doc: BatchScheduler configures which batch scheduler will be used for scheduling

fields.name: TimeToLiveSeconds
fields.doc: TimeToLiveSeconds defines the Time-To-Live (TTL) duration in seconds for this SparkApplication after its termination. The SparkApplication object will be garbage collected if the current time is more than the TimeToLiveSeconds since its termination.

fields.name: BatchSchedulerOptions
fields.doc: BatchSchedulerOptions provides fine-grained control on how to batch scheduling.

fields.name: SparkUIOptions
fields.doc: SparkUIOptions allows configuring the Service and the Ingress to expose the sparkUI

fields.name: DriverIngressOptions
fields.doc: DriverIngressOptions allows configuring the Service and the Ingress to expose ports inside Spark Driver

fields.name: DynamicAllocation
fields.doc: DynamicAllocation configures dynamic allocation that becomes available for the Kubernetes scheduler backend since Spark 3.0.

api_types.name: DriverIngressConfiguration
api_types.doc: DriverIngressConfiguration is for driver ingress specific configuration parameters.

fields.name: ServicePort
fields.doc: ServicePort allows configuring the port at service level that might be different from the targetPort.

fields.name: ServicePortName
fields.doc: ServicePortName allows configuring the name of the service port. This may be useful for sidecar proxies like Envoy injected by Istio which require specific ports names to treat traffic as proper HTTP.

fields.name: ServiceType
fields.doc: ServiceType allows configuring the type of the service. Defaults to ClusterIP.

fields.name: ServiceAnnotations
fields.doc: ServiceAnnotations is a map of key,value pairs of annotations that might be added to the service object.

fields.name: ServiceLabels
fields.doc: ServiceLabels is a map of key,value pairs of labels that might be added to the service object.

fields.name: IngressURLFormat
fields.doc: IngressURLFormat is the URL for the ingress.

fields.name: IngressAnnotations
fields.doc: IngressAnnotations is a map of key,value pairs of annotations that might be added to the ingress object. i.e. specify nginx as ingress.class

fields.name: IngressTLS
fields.doc: TlsHosts is useful If we need to declare SSL certificates to the ingress object

api_types.name: ApplicationState
api_types.doc: ApplicationState tells the current state of the application and an error message in case of failures.

api_types.name: Dependencies
api_types.doc: Dependencies specifies all possible types of dependencies of a Spark application.

fields.name: Jars
fields.doc: Jars is a list of JAR files the Spark application depends on.

fields.name: Files
fields.doc: Files is a list of files the Spark application depends on.

fields.name: PyFiles
fields.doc: PyFiles is a list of Python files the Spark application depends on.

fields.name: Packages
fields.doc: Packages is a list of maven coordinates of jars to include on the driver and executor classpaths. This will search the local maven repo, then maven central and any additional remote repositories given by the "repositories" option. Each package should be of the form "groupId:artifactId:version".

fields.name: ExcludePackages
fields.doc: ExcludePackages is a list of "groupId:artifactId", to exclude while resolving the dependencies provided in Packages to avoid dependency conflicts.

fields.name: Repositories
fields.doc: Repositories is a list of additional remote repositories to search for the maven coordinate given with the "packages" option.

fields.name: Archives
fields.doc: Archives is a list of archives to be extracted into the working directory of each executor.

api_types.name: SparkPodSpec
api_types.doc: SparkPodSpec defines common things that can be customized for a Spark driver or executor pod. TODO: investigate if we should use v1.PodSpec and limit what can be set instead.

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is a pod template that can be used to define the driver or executor pod configurations that Spark configurations do not support. Spark version >= 3.0.0 is required. Ref: https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/running-on-kubernetes.html#pod-template.

fields.name: Cores
fields.doc: Cores maps to `spark.driver.cores` or `spark.executor.cores` for the driver and executors, respectively.

fields.name: CoreLimit
fields.doc: CoreLimit specifies a hard limit on CPU cores for the pod. Optional

fields.name: Memory
fields.doc: Memory is the amount of memory to request for the pod.

fields.name: MemoryLimit
fields.doc: MemoryLimit overrides the memory limit of the pod.

fields.name: MemoryOverhead
fields.doc: MemoryOverhead is the amount of off-heap memory to allocate in cluster mode, in MiB unless otherwise specified.

fields.name: GPU
fields.doc: GPU specifies GPU requirement for the pod.

fields.name: Image
fields.doc: Image is the container image to use. Overrides Spec.Image if set.

fields.name: ConfigMaps
fields.doc: ConfigMaps carries information of other ConfigMaps to add to the pod.

fields.name: Secrets
fields.doc: Secrets carries information of secrets to add to the pod.

fields.name: Env
fields.doc: Env carries the environment variables to add to the pod.

fields.name: EnvVars
fields.doc: EnvVars carries the environment variables to add to the pod. Deprecated. Consider using `env` instead.

fields.name: EnvFrom
fields.doc: EnvFrom is a list of sources to populate environment variables in the container.

fields.name: EnvSecretKeyRefs
fields.doc: EnvSecretKeyRefs holds a mapping from environment variable names to SecretKeyRefs. Deprecated. Consider using `env` instead.

fields.name: Labels
fields.doc: Labels are the Kubernetes labels to be added to the pod.

fields.name: Annotations
fields.doc: Annotations are the Kubernetes annotations to be added to the pod.

fields.name: VolumeMounts
fields.doc: VolumeMounts specifies the volumes listed in ".spec.volumes" to mount into the main container's filesystem.

fields.name: Affinity
fields.doc: Affinity specifies the affinity/anti-affinity settings for the pod.

fields.name: Tolerations
fields.doc: Tolerations specifies the tolerations listed in ".spec.tolerations" to be applied to the pod.

fields.name: PodSecurityContext
fields.doc: PodSecurityContext specifies the PodSecurityContext to apply.

fields.name: SecurityContext
fields.doc: SecurityContext specifies the container's SecurityContext to apply.

fields.name: SchedulerName
fields.doc: SchedulerName specifies the scheduler that will be used for scheduling

fields.name: Sidecars
fields.doc: Sidecars is a list of sidecar containers that run along side the main Spark container.

fields.name: InitContainers
fields.doc: InitContainers is a list of init-containers that run to completion before the main Spark container.

fields.name: HostNetwork
fields.doc: HostNetwork indicates whether to request host networking for the pod or not.

fields.name: NodeSelector
fields.doc: NodeSelector is the Kubernetes node selector to be added to the driver and executor pods. This field is mutually exclusive with nodeSelector at SparkApplication level (which will be deprecated).

fields.name: DNSConfig
fields.doc: DnsConfig dns settings for the pod, following the Kubernetes specifications.

fields.name: TerminationGracePeriodSeconds
fields.doc: Termination grace period seconds for the pod

fields.name: ServiceAccount
fields.doc: ServiceAccount is the name of the custom Kubernetes service account used by the pod.

fields.name: HostAliases
fields.doc: HostAliases settings for the pod, following the Kubernetes specifications.

fields.name: ShareProcessNamespace
fields.doc: ShareProcessNamespace settings for the pod, following the Kubernetes specifications.

api_types.name: DriverSpec
api_types.doc: DriverSpec is specification of the driver.

fields.name: PodName
fields.doc: PodName is the name of the driver pod that the user creates. This is used for the in-cluster client mode in which the user creates a client pod where the driver of the user application runs. It's an error to set this field if Mode is not in-cluster-client.

fields.name: CoreRequest
fields.doc: CoreRequest is the physical CPU core request for the driver. Maps to `spark.kubernetes.driver.request.cores` that is available since Spark 3.0.

fields.name: JavaOptions
fields.doc: JavaOptions is a string of extra JVM options to pass to the driver. For instance, GC settings or other logging.

fields.name: Lifecycle
fields.doc: Lifecycle for running preStop or postStart commands

fields.name: KubernetesMaster
fields.doc: KubernetesMaster is the URL of the Kubernetes master used by the driver to manage executor pods and other Kubernetes resources. Default to https://kubernetes.default.svc.

fields.name: ServiceAnnotations
fields.doc: ServiceAnnotations defines the annotations to be added to the Kubernetes headless service used by executors to connect to the driver.

fields.name: ServiceLabels
fields.doc: ServiceLabels defines the labels to be added to the Kubernetes headless service used by executors to connect to the driver.

fields.name: Ports
fields.doc: Ports settings for the pods, following the Kubernetes specifications.

fields.name: PriorityClassName
fields.doc: PriorityClassName is the name of the PriorityClass for the driver pod.

api_types.name: ExecutorSpec
api_types.doc: ExecutorSpec is specification of the executor.

fields.name: Instances
fields.doc: Instances is the number of executor instances.

fields.name: CoreRequest
fields.doc: CoreRequest is the physical CPU core request for the executors. Maps to `spark.kubernetes.executor.request.cores` that is available since Spark 2.4.

fields.name: JavaOptions
fields.doc: JavaOptions is a string of extra JVM options to pass to the executors. For instance, GC settings or other logging.

fields.name: Lifecycle
fields.doc: Lifecycle for running preStop or postStart commands

fields.name: DeleteOnTermination
fields.doc: DeleteOnTermination specify whether executor pods should be deleted in case of failure or normal termination. Maps to `spark.kubernetes.executor.deleteOnTermination` that is available since Spark 3.0.

fields.name: Ports
fields.doc: Ports settings for the pods, following the Kubernetes specifications.

fields.name: PriorityClassName
fields.doc: PriorityClassName is the name of the PriorityClass for the executor pod.

api_types.name: NamePath
api_types.doc: NamePath is a pair of a name and a path to which the named objects should be mounted to.

api_types.name: DriverInfo
api_types.doc: DriverInfo captures information about the driver.

fields.name: WebUIAddress
fields.doc: UI Details for the UI created via ClusterIP service accessible from within the cluster.

fields.name: WebUIIngressName
fields.doc: Ingress Details if an ingress for the UI was created.

api_types.name: SecretInfo
api_types.doc: SecretInfo captures information of a secret.

api_types.name: NameKey
api_types.doc: NameKey represents the name and key of a SecretKeyRef.

api_types.name: Port
api_types.doc: Port represents the port definition in the pods objects.

api_types.name: MonitoringSpec
api_types.doc: MonitoringSpec defines the monitoring specification.

fields.name: ExposeDriverMetrics
fields.doc: ExposeDriverMetrics specifies whether to expose metrics on the driver.

fields.name: ExposeExecutorMetrics
fields.doc: ExposeExecutorMetrics specifies whether to expose metrics on the executors.

fields.name: MetricsProperties
fields.doc: MetricsProperties is the content of a custom metrics.properties for configuring the Spark metric system. If not specified, the content in spark-docker/conf/metrics.properties will be used.

fields.name: MetricsPropertiesFile
fields.doc: MetricsPropertiesFile is the container local path of file metrics.properties for configuring the Spark metric system. If not specified, value /etc/metrics/conf/metrics.properties will be used.

fields.name: Prometheus
fields.doc: Prometheus is for configuring the Prometheus JMX exporter.

api_types.name: PrometheusSpec
api_types.doc: PrometheusSpec defines the Prometheus specification when Prometheus is to be used for collecting and exposing metrics.

fields.name: JmxExporterJar
fields.doc: JmxExporterJar is the path to the Prometheus JMX exporter jar in the container.

fields.name: Port
fields.doc: Port is the port of the HTTP server run by the Prometheus JMX exporter. If not specified, 8090 will be used as the default.

fields.name: PortName
fields.doc: PortName is the port name of prometheus JMX exporter port. If not specified, jmx-exporter will be used as the default.

fields.name: ConfigFile
fields.doc: ConfigFile is the path to the custom Prometheus configuration file provided in the Spark image. ConfigFile takes precedence over Configuration, which is shown below.

fields.name: Configuration
fields.doc: Configuration is the content of the Prometheus configuration needed by the Prometheus JMX exporter. If not specified, the content in spark-docker/conf/prometheus.yaml will be used. Configuration has no effect if ConfigFile is set.

fields.name: Name
fields.doc: Name is GPU resource name, such as: nvidia.com/gpu or amd.com/gpu

fields.name: Quantity
fields.doc: Quantity is the number of GPUs to request for driver or executor.

api_types.name: DynamicAllocation
api_types.doc: DynamicAllocation contains configuration options for dynamic allocation.

fields.name: Enabled
fields.doc: Enabled controls whether dynamic allocation is enabled or not.

fields.name: InitialExecutors
fields.doc: InitialExecutors is the initial number of executors to request. If .spec.executor.instances is also set, the initial number of executors is set to the bigger of that and this option.

fields.name: MinExecutors
fields.doc: MinExecutors is the lower bound for the number of executors if dynamic allocation is enabled.

fields.name: MaxExecutors
fields.doc: MaxExecutors is the upper bound for the number of executors if dynamic allocation is enabled.

fields.name: ShuffleTrackingEnabled
fields.doc: ShuffleTrackingEnabled enables shuffle file tracking for executors, which allows dynamic allocation without the need for an external shuffle service. This option will try to keep alive executors that are storing shuffle data for active jobs. If external shuffle service is enabled, set ShuffleTrackingEnabled to false. ShuffleTrackingEnabled is true by default if dynamicAllocation.enabled is true.

fields.name: ShuffleTrackingTimeout
fields.doc: ShuffleTrackingTimeout controls the timeout in milliseconds for executors that are holding shuffle data if shuffle tracking is enabled (true by default if dynamic allocation is enabled).

operator_config.doc:
SparkSubmitter implements SparkApplicationSubmitter interface.
This interface is highly experimental and may go under significant changes or removed in the future.

summary: spark-operator is a Kubernetes component. managing 3 CRD(s): ScheduledSparkApplication, SparkApplication, SparkConnect. deploying 3 workload(s) (spark-operator-webhook, controller-manager, spark-operator-controller) with 3 container(s). watching 1 resource type(s). exposing 1 service(s). with 5 webhook(s) (2 validating, 3 mutating). protected by 1 network policy/policies. defining 12 API spec type(s) across 35 struct(s) with 220 total fields. with 24 operator config constants. reporting 4 status condition types. with 1 ingress route(s).
